nativeRange.habitat: Inshore reef environments with mixed algae and coral formations; also found along coastal drop-offs at 3–30 m depth

diet.notes: Wild diet consists primarily of sponges, colonial ascidians, and hydroids; it is considered a sponge specialist. In captivity it can sometimes be transitioned to meaty foods including live or frozen brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, and other small invertebrates, but replicating the sponge-heavy natural diet is difficult and feeding reluctance is common. Its elongated snout is adapted for probing crevices in search of food; pressing food into rock crevices has been reported to encourage feeding in captivity.

summary: The Hi Fin Coradion Butterflyfish (Coradion altivelis) is a silver-white Indo-Pacific species marked by dark brown vertical bars and a yellow-orange posterior band. An expert-level fish whose wild diet centers on sponges and ascidians, it is not considered reef safe and is best kept singly or as a mated pair.
careNotes:
The Hi Fin Coradion Butterflyfish (Coradion altivelis) is considered uncommon in the aquarium trade and is generally regarded as an expert-level species, largely because of how difficult it is to feed in captivity. In the wild it feeds primarily on sponges, colonial ascidians (filter-feeding invertebrates that resemble sponges), and hydroids — food items that cannot be replicated in most home aquariums. Some specimens can be coaxed onto live or frozen brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, and other meaty foods, and pressing food into rock crevices has been reported to encourage initial feeding, but long-term success with this species remains rare.

The Hi Fin Coradion Butterflyfish is not considered reef safe. It is documented to nip at corals and will consume tube worms, clams, and small crustaceans, making it incompatible with invertebrate-stocked reef systems.

It tends to be territorial toward its own kind and other members of the same genus, so it is generally advised to keep only one specimen per tank, or a confirmed male/female pair. A minimum tank volume of around 500 L is recommended to accommodate its territorial behavior and active foraging. Tank setup should include ample live rockwork with crevices for the fish to explore and shelter in, along with open areas for swimming.
breedingNotes: The Hi Fin Coradion Butterflyfish is oviparous (egg-laying) and forms pair bonds during the breeding season, consistent with the monogamous pairing behavior typical of butterflyfishes in the family Chaetodontidae. Captive breeding has not been documented in the hobby literature.

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing (6 in vs LiveAquaria 7 in), tank size (100 gal vs 125 gal), temperament, and reef safe status. QM rates this species explicitly not reef-safe due to predation on corals and invertebrates; LiveAquaria rates 'with caution'. Disagreement noted; dataStatus set to needs_review.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (family Chaetodontidae, order Acanthuriformes), max size (18 cm TL), wild temperature range (23.5–29.3°C), native range (Indo-West Pacific), and confirmation of oviparous pair-bonding reproduction.

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Rates as 'often reef safe'; notes tubeworm and clam consumption; minimum tank 400 L. Considered an outlier relative to other sources on reef safety.

additional.name: Reeflex
additional.notes: Rates not reef safe; notes sponge specialist diet; recommends 1000 L minimum; expert care level.

additional.name: Wikipedia – Coradion altivelis
additional.notes: Confirms sponges are major component of wild diet; also consumes colonial ascidians and hydroids.

fish.breedingNotes: No documented captive breeding; forms pair bonds in the wild and is oviparous.
